failure_mode_description: Denial factual error — week count. Denial asserts a specific factual claim (PT duration) that a single sentence in the clinical record directly contradicts. No regulatory override needed. Pure factual verification.
anchor_fact: Denial says 3 weeks PT. PT summary field says 9 weeks total (Valley PT 3 weeks + Capital PT 6 weeks). The PT summary field is explicitly labelled as authoritative.

procedure.requested_by: Dr. Sarah Okonkwo, MD — Spine Surgery

denial.reason_text: Member does not meet conservative treatment requirements under CC-SPINE-2024 Section 2.2. Physical therapy: member has completed only 3 weeks of physical therapy. Standard pathway requires 6 weeks minimum. Functional plateau not documented. All other criteria remain unmet.
denial.reviewer: Dr. Alan Foster, MD — HealthFirst Utilization Management
denial.correct_fact: 9 weeks PT completed across two providers

appeal_basis: Denial miscounts PT — member completed 3 weeks at Valley PT and 6 weeks at Capital PT, totaling 9 weeks
Both PT providers' records were submitted with the original authorization request
PT discharge from Capital PT explicitly documents functional plateau
All criteria are met — pharmacotherapy and injection also completed

mri_1.findings: C6-C7 large disc herniation with moderate right C7 nerve root compression. No cord contact. No myelomalacia. Right foraminal stenosis confirmed.

neurological_exam.findings: Right C7 dermatomal numbness. Tricep reflex diminished right. Grip strength 4/5 right. No bilateral findings. No UMN signs.

physical_therapy_provider_2.outcome: Plateau reached. Discharge summary: 'Maximum therapeutic benefit achieved. No further improvement expected from continued PT.'

physical_therapy_summary.plateau_source: Capital PT discharge summary — explicit language: maximum therapeutic benefit achieved
physical_therapy_summary.note: 9 weeks total PT across two providers. Functional plateau explicitly documented at Capital PT discharge. Both records submitted with original PA request.

agents.response: Partial pain relief, radicular symptoms persist

right_c7_epidural.response: 35% temporary relief, returned to baseline in 3 weeks

documents_enclosed: Capital PT records (6 weeks) with plateau notation
question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C6-C7 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — denial says 3 weeks PT, need 6. Criteria not met.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: The denial claims 3 weeks PT. The clinical record's PT summary field says 9 weeks across two providers. Which is correct?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Both PT records were submitted with the original PA request. Did the plan reviewer read both records?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: With 9 weeks PT and documented plateau, are all criteria met?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Does any regulatory override or clinical standard issue exist here, or is this purely a factual error correction?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: The denial's factual claim (3 weeks PT) is directly contradicted by the clinical record's PT summary (9 weeks total). A system that accepts the denial's factual assertion without checking the clinical record upholds. A system that reads the clinical record against the denial catches the discrepancy and overturns on criteria met. This is a pure factual verification test with no regulatory complexity. The anchor is the denial's specific numerical claim.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — The denial's factual basis is wrong. The clinical record documents 9 weeks of PT across two providers (Valley PT: 3 weeks, Capital PT: 6 weeks), with functional plateau explicitly documented at Capital PT discharge. All criteria under CC-SPINE-2024 Section 2.2 are met: 9 weeks PT exceeds the 6-week minimum, plateau is documented, two pharmacotherapy agents trialed, one interventional procedure completed. The denial reviewer counted only the Valley PT records and missed the Capital PT records entirely.

fm1_anchor_correction.score_2: Determination explicitly states the denial's PT count is wrong, cites 9 weeks from both providers with source
fm1_anchor_correction.score_1: Determination reaches OVERTURN but does not explicitly name the factual error (just says criteria met)
fm1_anchor_correction.score_0: Determination accepts 3-week count and upholds, or reaches OVERTURN without correcting the stated PT figure
